versions.name: Table Lookups, Custom Tables, More Formula Helpers
changes: New: lookup(table, keyCol, keyVal, returnCol) formula helper - VLOOKUP-style, finds the first row where keyCol equals keyVal and returns its returnCol value. Works on any scanned table or a custom table.

New: custom tables in the Tables tab - hand-entered lookup data (e.g. part numbers, cost centers) that doesn't come from a Maximo scan, readable by formulas with the exact same T()/col()/has()/lookup() functions as a scanned table.
New formula helpers: count(table, colName, value), ifBlank(val, fallback), trim/upper/lower(str), left/right(str, n), mid(str, start, len), sum(arr)/avg(arr), today(), and daysBetween(a, b).

New (opt-in): whoami(field) returns the current user's Maximo profile field (username/email/displayName/insertSite/country/langcode) in a formula - off by default (Settings > Display > "Allow whoami() in formulas"), since a rule using it could paste your name/email into a permanent WO record.
Feedback tab: added an opt-in checkbox to include your Maximo name/username/email with a report - off by default, never fetched unless checked.

Fixed: a group's linked table showing 0 rows because its owning scan step intentionally didn't run this pass (a false condition) no longer shows a red error - only a genuine capture failure does now, with a plain, friendly message instead of the raw table id/prefix string.

New beta feature (beta_2, experimental): domain(key, code) decodes a code via one of Maximo's cached domain lists; assetWOHistory(assetnum, siteid, limit) and assetDowntimeHistory(assetnum, siteid) pull an asset's work order / downtime history live from Maximo's own REST API. Data shapes and field reliability are still being verified - don't build a rule you depend on around these yet.

New (beta_2): API Tables in the Tables tab - a named table backed by a live REST lookup (asset work order or downtime history) instead of a scan or hand-typed rows, whose asset/site come from their own formulas so the same table definition follows whichever WO you're reviewing. Readable by T()/lookup()/col() exactly like any scanned or custom table.
New: a Formula Reference button (book icon, next to Save in Setup) opens a searchable in-app list of every formula function, so you don't need to open the Guide in a separate tab.
New: typing a bare function name anywhere in a formula box now suggests matching functions to insert - previously autocomplete only helped once you'd already typed a function's opening parenthesis.

versions.name: Formula Autocomplete, Custom Dialogs, Tables Tab, Text Cleanup
changes: New: every formula/condition field (Rules, Variables, Scan targets/actions, Row Detail Fields) now offers F(/T(/V( completion (fields, tables, variables) and an Excel-style signature tooltip for every helper function (col, has, hours, hoursBetween, oneOf, contains, matches, isEmpty, notEmpty, maxLaborHours) as you type.

New: message boxes (Short/Long/custom return message) show an inline warning if they contain a formula/variable call that isn't wrapped in {{ }} - that text would otherwise just show up literally instead of being evaluated.

New: a Tables Setup tab lists every table referenced by Groups/Scan and lets you give it a friendly display name (the downtime history table ships with one built in) - the raw table stays untouched, this only changes what's displayed.
New: every delete action (Rules, Groups, Variables, Scan targets, Profiles) now asks for confirmation first.
Every native browser alert/confirm/prompt popup has been replaced with the tool's own dark-themed dialogs, matching the rest of the UI.

Return, Approve, Fix, and Scan now share a busy lock - the buttons dim and hotkeys no-op while one of them is already running, closing a real risk of two actions firing at once (e.g. Approve firing mid-Return).

Reduced text throughout Settings, buttons, confirmation dialogs, tooltips, and status messages for a more compact interface - colors/icons now carry meaning that used to be repeated in words (e.g. a green check no longer also says "OK", an N/A rule dims instead of showing "N/A" text).
Default group/rule titles are shorter on brand-new installs (e.g. "Work Order Summary" - "Summary") - existing configs are unaffected and can still be renamed freely in Setup.
Setup's Save & Apply button is now just "Save" - hovering it shows which areas (Rules/Groups/Scan/Settings) actually have pending changes.
Closing Setup with unsaved changes now shows a custom Cancel/Save/Discard & Exit dialog instead of just closing silently.
The kebab menu's tooltip-editor item now reads "Set Tooltip" when an entry has none yet, "Edit Tooltip" once one exists.
The update-available banner has a new button to re-enable automatic (or auto-patch-only) updates in one click, if you'd previously turned that off.
Removed the "(var)" suffix from variable field labels - the blue label color already distinguishes them from real Maximo fields.
Fixed: the panel could get stuck reopening collapsed on every page load after collapsing it once - it now always opens expanded (the collapse button/hotkey still work for the rest of that session).
Fixed: the Save button's dirty-tracking flagged an already-saved Settings change (e.g. toggling a Beta feature) as still pending.
Fixed: switching profiles, saving as a new profile, or starting a blank profile could silently drop unsaved edits made in Setup.

Fixed: the version picker's "Latest" option reflected your own dev/beta access level rather than the update channel you actually had selected, so it could show a version your channel wouldn't actually install.

Update checking: if a pinned or channel-tracked version gets trimmed from this file's list, the tool now falls back to the nearest still-available version instead of erroring or silently jumping - always reported on the status line, never silent.
Version picker entries can now be gated to a specific grant (dev/beta).
Halved the visual gap between the Route symbol and the Return button.

versions.name: Fix Clipped Descenders in Card Headers
changes: Fixed: card header titles (Rules, Groups, Variables, Scan, and Settings cards) clipped the bottom of letters with descenders (g, y, p, q, j) - the fixed-height header box's line spacing didn't leave them enough room.

versions.name: Route Icon Fix + Layout Tweaks
changes: Fixed: the Route symbol's icon artwork only used a fraction of its own viewBox, so it rendered shrunk into one corner with a lot of dead space around it - it's now cropped tight so it fills its space properly.
Reverted the Route/Fix/Approve button row to its original height - the bigger Route icon from the last release was stretching the whole row taller.
The Fix button is narrower now instead of sharing equal width with Return/Approve.
Removed a redundant sentence from the Beta tab's intro text.
Fixed: the "..." overflow menu for Guide/Feedback/Export/Import (when Setup is too narrow) opened and immediately closed again in the same click - it stayed open like every other menu.

versions.name: Editable Return Message + New Default Hotkeys
changes: New: the return-message box is now editable - whatever's in it when you hit Return or Copy (button or hotkey) is exactly what gets used, not a value recomputed behind your back.
New: Alt+C hotkey to copy the return message, alongside Scan and Return.

Changed default hotkeys: Scan is now Alt+S (was Ctrl+Shift+S - if you never customized it, your Scan hotkey just changed), Return now defaults to Alt+R. Approve still has no default. Check Settings > Hotkeys if any of these conflict with something else you use.
Fixed: the Fix button's wrench showed as a color emoji that didn't match the rest of the tool's icons - it's now a plain line-art icon like everything else.
The Route symbol is significantly larger (was still too small after the last pass) - note the whole button row is now a bit taller to match.

versions.name: Entry Tooltips + Setup Polish
changes: New: Rules, Groups, Variables, and Scan entries can each have a plain-English tooltip (info icon next to the title, visible on hover) - set via the kebab menu's new "Edit Tooltip" action, or Groups' existing inline Tooltip field.
New: when the Setup window gets too narrow for the tab bar even after icon-shrinking every tab, Guide/Feedback/Export/Import collapse into a single "..." menu instead of overflowing off the edge.
Save & Apply no longer closes Setup - it saves, refreshes the active tab in place, and stays open.
Save & Apply now greys out automatically whenever there's nothing to save.

Fixed: the return-message Copy button could copy a stale/previous message before the first scan instead of what's actually shown - it's now disabled until there's something real to copy, and always copies exactly what's displayed.
Fixed: a return message prefix/suffix (greeting/signature) no longer appears on its own with no actual message in between.
Fixed: a floating version pin (e.g. "0.20.x") whose whole line got trimmed from the changelog was silently jumping to the latest version instead of saying so.
All hotkeys now live under one "Hotkeys" section in Settings instead of one card each.
Post-Scan Actions (the whole feature, not just "Run on") is now gated behind the same beta flag as Fix, since both are the same untested code path.
Beta tab: removed the explanation text, added a compact "Your access" line; Fix button is now yellow with a wrench icon; the Route symbol is larger.
Settings now has a Reset/Uninstall section: reset the tool while keeping your config, or erase everything for a fresh start.
Every tooltip in the tool (including the ones that were still using the browser's native hover text) now uses the same floating style.

versions.name: Audit Fixes
changes: Fixed: toggling a beta feature in the Beta tab, then clicking Save & Apply, silently reverted the toggle - it wrote to a different in-memory copy of settings than the one Save & Apply actually persisted.
Fixed: the 15-minute instant-launch cache still paid for a /bootstrap network round trip it didn't need - now a valid cache skips that too, not just whoami/check-access/tool-fetch.

Hardened: hotkeys (Scan/Return/Approve/Fix) no longer fire while typing in one of this tool's own text fields - this mattered more once Fix could silently overwrite field values with no confirmation dialog.

versions.name: Faster Access Checks + Issue-Tracked Feedback
changes: Access checks are much faster: the Worker now caches permissions.json and the tool source at the edge (pinned versions cache for a day since a tag never changes; the branch/dev channel caches for 15 seconds) instead of re-fetching from GitHub on every single click.

Repeat clicks within 15 minutes now skip the network entirely and run the already-cached tool with the already-cached grants - a deliberate speed/freshness tradeoff: a revoke can take up to 15 minutes to land on a browser that already cached a grant, instead of the very next click.

Feedback tab now files a GitHub Issue directly (private repo, routed through the Worker) instead of opening an email draft, and includes more context automatically: browser info, current channel/pin, and the last status line. Falls back to email if the report can't be filed for any reason.

versions.name: Minor Version 1 + Beta Framework
changes: Permissions now grant a LIST of flags per user instead of one tier (e.g. dev + beta_0 at once) - beta_0 is a wildcard for every beta feature, beta_1/beta_2/etc. gate one specific feature each.
New: Return and Approve can each have their own hotkey (Scan already could) - assigning a key combo already used by another action is now rejected instead of silently overwriting it.
New: Feedback tab in Setup to send a bug report or suggestion by email, pre-filled with version/grants for context.
Install tab is now only visible with the dev grant.
Drag handle icon is a bit wider and taller.
Startup status now shows a second line naming any active dev/beta grants, so access is visible every launch, not just discoverable via console.

Groups: a header message tied to a specific rule can now be left on "Auto" to always show whichever of the group's rules currently has the worst status (error > fail > warn > pass), instead of requiring one rule to be hand-picked ahead of time.

New Beta tab (visible only if you hold a beta grant) lists whichever beta features you qualify for with individual enable/disable toggles. Off means zero trace anywhere in the tool - no settings, no buttons, no menu items.

First beta feature (dev + beta_1 only, off by default): adds a neutral Route symbol and a Fix action next to Return/Approve. Fix reruns the scan and reapplies fields, and post-scan actions can now be limited to run only on Scan, only on Fix, or both.

versions.name: Multi-Company Redirect Fix
changes: Fixed a real bug in loader.js: when clicking the bookmarklet from an unrecognized page with more than one company's Maximo configured, it always redirected to the FIRST configured company regardless of which one you actually belonged to. With only one company configured this was invisible, but it would have silently misdirected anyone from a second company.

Now: one company configured behaves exactly as before (auto-redirect, no extra step). More than one configured and not yet on any known host shows a one-time "which Maximo instance is this for?" picker, remembers the choice, and redirects correctly from then on.

versions.name: Preserve Config Across Revoke
changes: A revoke no longer deletes your rules/groups/scan/settings outright - they're snapshotted locally first, then restored automatically the next time access is granted again on the same browser. Previously a revoke just wiped everything, with no way back short of a file-linked backup.
This applies to both revoke paths: the bookmarklet's first-load access check, and the tool's own periodic self-update re-check.

versions.name: Access-Controlled Updates
changes: The tool's own self-update mechanism (checking for and installing new versions, and the dev-channel tracker) now goes through the access-control Worker instead of fetching directly from a public URL - every update re-validates access, not just the bookmarklet's first load. A revoked user's already-running tool can no longer just keep self-updating forever with no check.

If a self-update check comes back as a confirmed access revoke (not just a network hiccup), the tool now tears itself down and clears its local config on the spot, instead of silently continuing to run.
Version pinning, channel selection, and the changelog banner all still work exactly as before - only where the actual source comes from changed.

versions.name: Config Version Control
changes: Config version control is now actually wired up - configVersion was stored on every profile but never read; there's now a real migration mechanism (CURRENT_CONFIG_VERSION + a version-keyed migration table) that runs on every profile load/import and stamps the result. No migrations exist yet since only one config shape has ever shipped, but the plumbing is live
version.json entries can now carry an optional display name shown alongside the version number in the update banner and the version picker dropdown
Groups tab: hiding an entry now also collapses it; showing an entry no longer forces it open, it just leaves the expand state alone
Fixed Groups tab: clicking the hidden button to unhide a group no longer also expands/collapses that entry - Show/Hide now only ever touches visibility

Main panel: starting a drag on a group tile no longer instantly snaps other expanded tiles shut - they now collapse smoothly, and the reorder itself doesn't engage until that settles, instead of both happening at once before you've even moved the mouse
Main panel: clicking a group's header to expand/collapse it now animates smoothly too, not just during drag

Settings version picker: dropped the "(auto-patch)" wording from each minor-version entry, and the default "Latest" option now shows the actual latest version number instead of just saying "current version"
Fixed a second spot where the textarea resize-grip's box artifact could still show up - the grip icon has its own background separate from the scrollbar corner, which wasn't covered before

Fixed the drag-reorder gap bug: when dragging a card past a neighbor, the displaced neighbor now lands exactly in the dragged card's slot immediately (previously it stopped short by the card's own margin until you released the drag)
Expanding/collapsing a card (by clicking its header) now animates smoothly instead of snapping instantly
Moving a card with the up/down arrow buttons now animates the swap instead of jumping instantly to the new order

Main panel group badges can now represent more than one rule at once instead of collapsing straight to the worst status: 2 rules split the badge in half with symbols; 3+ rules show the worst rule up front with the rest fanned out behind it as narrow color slivers - always the same total width as a single-rule badge
Fixed Exit button not fully closing the tool - it now also closes Setup (and the field-browser popup) if either was left open, instead of only removing the main panel
Renamed "Shared Presets" to "Import Shared Presets" in the Profiles tab
Fixed a bright square artifact around the resize-grip corner of textareas once a scrollbar appears - it now blends into the field's own background
Added a little breathing room between each Scan tab table column header and its info-icon tooltip

Scan tab tables (Row Detail Fields / Post-Scan Actions): narrow columns like Column Name/Element ID/Table Prefix/Expand Col no longer eat more width than they need, freeing up room for Collect Condition/Value Expression. Columns now shrink responsively as the Setup window narrows, and headers wrap at word boundaries onto a 2nd line instead of overflowing; below a sensible floor width the table scrolls horizontally instead of mangling text

Settings tab version picker: default option renamed to "Latest (current version)", and the per-minor-version grouping no longer uses an unselectable optgroup header - the "X.Y.x (auto-patch)" entry is now itself a selectable option, with exact patch versions indented underneath it
Main panel status badges (PASS/FAIL/WARN/ERR) are now a uniform fixed width instead of WARN rendering wider than the others
Setup modal corners are now square while snapped to any screen edge, instead of showing rounded corners flush against the edge
Guide button now points to the tool's GitHub Pages guide instead of the old Netlify URL
Scan tab: Tab ID / Wait for text / Wait for table fields are no longer crammed on one line - each has its own label and room to breathe
Added a "Scan Order" label under WO Tab ID to make clear the list below is the scan sequence
Move up/down tooltips are now just "Move up"/"Move down" - dropped the "runs earlier/later" wording

Row Detail Fields and Post-Scan Actions are now real tables - one header row instead of repeating every field's label on every single entry, with a small info icon on each column header explaining what it's for. Both tables share the same layout/style. This also fixes Expand Col's 2-line header pushing it out of alignment with Table Prefix/Collect Condition, since headers now live in their own row
Add field / Add action buttons are bigger and easier to hit
Delete buttons (Scan tab row/action tables, Groups tab field rows, Local Profiles) now use the same trash-can icon as the kebab menu's Delete, instead of a plain X or text button
Local Profiles: Switch is now disabled whenever it's your only saved profile (previously it could still be clicked even though it would do nothing)
Renamed "Import Preset from GitHub" to "Shared Presets" and removed "GitHub" from the re-import confirmation text

Main panel's group tiles now use the same click-and-drag reorder engine as Setup's Rules/Groups/Variables/Scan tabs, with other tiles visually collapsing and sliding out of the way, instead of the old native drag-and-drop's plain border-highlight

Profiles, Settings, and Update tabs restyled to match the dark card theme used everywhere else in Setup - every section is now a collapsible card with the same header/button/input/dropdown styling instead of old hardcoded grey boxes and colors

Renamed the "Update" tab to "Install" - it's a manual paste-and-install tool for the raw script, which was easy to confuse with the automatic channel/version updater in the Settings tab's own "Updates" section; a note now also explains the distinction on that tab

Groups tab hide/unhide icon: hidden state is now a visibly darker, dimmer grey instead of a shade too close to the visible state; re-verified the unhide-also-toggles-expand / hide-doesn't behavior is wired correctly (a synthetic click test confirms only unhide toggles the header)

Setup window's snap-to-left live preview (shown while still dragging, before you drop) now shows the narrow reset width immediately when snapping fresh from a floating state, instead of showing the old remembered width right up until you actually let go

Dragging or resizing the Setup window now ends the gesture the moment the cursor leaves the browser window, instead of freezing mid-drag or silently ignoring a mouse-up that happens outside the window - there's no way to keep tracking a cursor once it's left the OS window, so leaving is now treated as the release point (same effect as letting go right at the edge)

Variables and Scan tabs rebuilt to match Rules/Groups: card headers with a drag handle, move up/down, and a Rename/Duplicate/Delete kebab menu instead of a lone Delete button; Scan's Row Detail Fields and Post-Scan Actions sub-editors restyled with the shared dark theme and icon+tooltip add/delete buttons instead of hardcoded colors
Redesigned the drag-handle icon to an up chevron / two lines / down chevron (reads as a drag handle, not a generic arrow)

Fixed the drag handle and move up/down buttons showing on every row instead of only the one being hovered - the hide/reveal opacity now uses !important, since a host page's own broad rules can beat even a higher-specificity plain rule
Drag handle now reveals on hovering the card's header OR its expanded body (previously header-only), matching the move buttons
Move up/down buttons no longer show a background box on hover - just the icon itself brightens
At the top/bottom of a list, the move button for the impossible direction (e.g. move up on the first entry) is now omitted entirely instead of shown dimmed

Card headers across Rules/Groups/Variables/Scan are now hard-clamped to the same height (instead of relying on every row's content naturally computing the same size), to permanently rule out any tab's rows silently rendering thicker than another's
Move up/down buttons are now hidden until you hover the card (header or its expanded body) - just a plain icon that lights up on hover, no visible button box sitting there all the time
Added a small drag-handle indicator on the left of each card's header, appearing only while hovering that row - also nudges the title text slightly right to make room for it

Card drag-reorder now only needs to cross header-sized increments instead of a tall expanded card's full height - every other card temporarily collapses to its header while you're dragging (whether it's the dragged card or one being displaced), so reordering long expanded rows no longer requires dragging across their entire height. Real expand/collapse state is untouched and simply reappears once you drop

Fixed a real cursor-tracking bug in that same drag: collapsing a card above the one you're dragging used to yank the dragged card away from the cursor by however much space that collapse freed up - the drag now compensates so the card stays glued to the cursor

Main panel tooltips (the info and hide-group icons) were landing far from the icon whenever the icon sat near the panel's right edge, which is most of them since the panel is right-docked - a fixed 250px clamp was shoving the tooltip's whole box leftward instead of just keeping it on-screen; it now anchors to whichever side of the icon actually has room
Groups tab: hide/show button now has a hover tooltip; unhiding a group also toggles its expand/collapse state as a quick way to surface it again, hiding leaves it alone

Setup window: dragging to the left edge from a floating (non-snapped) state now always snaps to the narrowest width, even if a previous left-snap session had been resized wider - that remembered width is only for reopening Setup while still snapped left, not for re-snapping fresh
Hid the native scrollbar on the main panel's group list and added faint top/bottom scroll shadows that appear only when there's more content to scroll in that direction

Rules, Groups, Variables, and Scan cards can now be reordered by clicking and dragging their header - other cards visually slide out of the way by the exact height of the card you're dragging (expanded or collapsed), snapping into the new order on release

A plain click on a card header still just expands/collapses it as before - dragging is only armed once the mouse has moved a few pixels, so the two gestures never fight each other or misfire into one another

The reorder drag survives the mouse crossing over Maximo's own page content underneath the Setup window, using the same pointer-capture technique already used for dragging/resizing the Setup window itself
Groups tab dragging updates the same shared order the main panel's group tiles use, so both stay in sync
Added move up/down reorder buttons to Rules, Groups, and Variables tabs - sleek stroke-arrow icons (dimmed at the top/bottom of the list) instead of the old unicode triangle style
Scan tab's existing move up/down buttons replaced with the same sleek icon style as the other tabs
Rules and Variables tab order is already the single source of truth used everywhere they're referenced, so reordering there needed no extra wiring

Groups tab now displays and reorders in the same visual order as the main panel's group tiles (previously it showed/reordered cfg.groups' raw storage order, which silently diverged from the main panel the moment tiles had ever been drag-reordered there) - fixed by having the Groups tab read and write the same order the main panel already uses

Fixed a CSS specificity bug where the isolation reset rule (chained :not(svg):not(svg *)) could outrank plain type-selector rules declared later - this silently overrode the vertical-only textarea resize handle (Expanded Message box was still resizable both ways) and gave every <select> dropdown in Setup a native white background instead of the dark theme
Groups tab collapsed card headers are now the same height as Rules tab cards - the eye/hide toggle button was using bigger padding than the kebab button, puffing up the row
Kebab menu items (Rename/Duplicate/Delete) now actually highlight on hover - the hover color was identical to the menu's own background so it was doing nothing
+ Add Group button now matches the styling of + Add Rule
Setup window can now be resized wider/narrower from the east edge while pinned left, without unsnapping - Maximo keeps getting pushed to match the new width, and the width is remembered for next time

Groups & Display tab now matches Rules exactly: no more arrow triangle (click anywhere on the header to expand), plain title text instead of an always-editable boxed input, and a Rename/Duplicate/Delete kebab menu instead of a lone Delete button
Visible checkbox replaced with an eye/eye-slash icon toggle - bright white when shown, dimmed when hidden
Removed the Layout (Vertical/Horizontal) dropdown - it never actually affected anything, it was dead UI with no wiring behind it
Removed the separate "Variable Fields" checklist - a variable added there rendered identically to one added via a field row, so it was pure duplication of a feature that already exists
All Setup textareas (not just the Rules formula box) are now vertical-resize only
Groups tab cards now remember which ones are expanded across tab switches, matching Rules; both tabs also remember scroll position per tab until Setup is closed
The field-row editor's +field/x row text buttons are now plain +/x icons with hover tooltips
Browse Page Fields popup restyled to match the rest of Setup instead of its old flat grey look

Reverted the Return/Route experiment - Return and Approve are back to plain buttons, no icon (the route.txt icon isn't currently used anywhere; flagged to the user that neither button has an obviously better claim to it than the other)

Groups & Display tab restyled to match the dark card-based design used in Rules and the main panel - group cards, header-message and variable-fields sub-panels, the field-row editor, and every button/input on this tab now use the shared theme instead of old hardcoded grey colors
Return button is now Route, using a new node/route icon instead of the unicode arrow
Settings icon replaced with an 8-tooth flat-topped gear (Chrome/Material "settings" glyph shape) instead of the previous zigzag design

Fixed the active Setup tab's rounded corner-merge effect: the right corner was sitting higher than the left because it was inheriting leftover position styling from the tab-separator rule; both corners now align exactly, with a slightly larger radius

Lowered the Setup window's minimum width (420px to 360px) - the higher floor left a dead gap between the tab groups once every tab shrinks to icon-only, and blocked narrowing the window any further than that
Rule kebab dots (fixed in v0.20.16 to use stroke instead of fill) were rendered too thick and overlapping each other — resized to sit cleanly separated again; same fix applied to the info-icon dot
Setup tab bar: Rules through Update are now one visual group (no more odd gap at Scan/Profiles) — only Guide/Export/Import stays a separate group
Tab separators are now a short Chrome-style line through the middle of the tab, not a full-height border

Setup titlebar and the main panel header are now the same fixed height and font alignment, so they line up when the Setup window is snapped beside the docked panel — matches Maximo's own Carbon header height (queried live via .bx--header, falls back to 48px)
Dragging a snapped Setup window by its titlebar now reverts it to its standard (non-snapped) size immediately, instead of dragging around at the snapped size
Replaced the settings gear icon with a proper gear silhouette
Setup window can now be resized from any edge or corner, not just the bottom-right

Fixed every icon in the docked panel and Setup modal rendering invisible (no stroke/fill) — the CSS isolation reset added in v0.20.13 was also stripping the stroke="currentColor"/fill="currentColor" attributes the icons rely on; this was happening on every domain, not just IBM Maximo

Fixed the Setup window's drag/resize freezing over parts of the Maximo page — Maximo tiles the screen with iframes, and tracking mouse movement via `document` stops working once the pointer crosses into one; drag and resize now capture the pointer via a full-viewport overlay so they keep tracking regardless of what's underneath

Setup window now snaps like a normal OS window: drag it to the left edge (snaps to minimum width, pushes Maximo right, like the docked panel), right edge (snaps to cover the docked panel), top-left (fills the Maximo content area), or top-right (covers the whole browser). The last snap used is remembered and restored next time Setup is opened, and re-adjusts automatically if the browser is resized or moved to another display

Rules tab: an expanded rule now stays expanded when you switch to another Setup tab and back — it only re-collapses when Setup is closed and reopened, or when you click the Rules tab header while already on it
Rules tab kebab menu (Rename/Duplicate/Delete) is no longer artificially wide — it now sizes to its content
Fixed an odd-looking gap in the Setup tab bar between tab groups (e.g. between Scan and Profiles) — replaced with a deliberate divider line, matching the same treatment at every group boundary
Rule formula box in the Rules tab can no longer be dragged wider than the panel — only vertical resize is allowed now, matching every other textarea's width-locked-to-container behavior

Setup tabs now always fit on one row. Right-click a tab for a 4th option, Auto (in addition to Icon / Word / Icon + Word) — Auto tabs shrink to icon-only first as the modal narrows, lowest-priority (rightmost) tab first, escalating toward Rules only if still needed. Icon/Word pins are locked and never auto-adjust (even if that causes overflow); an Icon + Word pin is touched only as an absolute last resort.

This round (v0.20.13-0.20.14) is not yet verified live in a browser from this environment — please test on both Google and IBM Maximo domains, especially: does IBM now render the Setup modal with proper styling (Chrome-style tabs, dark menus, resize handle bottom-right)? Does resizing the modal shrink/grow tabs smoothly with no flicker or console errors? Does the Rules kebab menu open correctly on a collapsed rule?

Fixed a real bug from live testing: the Rules tab's new kebab menu was invisible on a collapsed rule (the default state) because it was clipped by the card's overflow:hidden — it now positions itself outside the card, like the tab display-mode menu already did

Added a CSS isolation reset for both the docked panel and Setup modal — host pages (especially IBM Maximo/Carbon) were bleeding their own button/input/svg styling through ours, which is the likely cause of several per-domain rendering differences reported (ancient-looking menus, missing Chrome-tab shape, resize handle in the wrong corner). Please re-test on the domains where things looked wrong before I chase remaining cosmetic issues individually.
Rule headers in the Rules tab now use a distinct background from the body, matching how group headers look on the main panel
Settings tab icon is now an actual gear/cog shape
Main panel: the grey hover pill (tooltip/hide icon) is now exactly the same size as the status badge it replaces, instead of being taller
Tooltips no longer show for text that's already fully visible — only when it's actually cut off (header messages), or when a Setup tab is shrunk to icon-only
Setup tab display-mode menu (Icon/Word/Icon+Word) and the Rules kebab menu are visually polished — vertical list, per-option icons, and a checkmark on the active display mode
Chrome-style tab corner curve is bigger/more visible; reduced the gap between tab groups
Return message box: text now uses the full width of the box — the text-only Copy button (which reserved 62px of padding) is now a minimalist icon button that briefly swaps to a checkmark on copy

Header hover area (tooltip/hide icon): now shows as a grey pill in the same spot the status badge occupies, with the icon(s) evenly centered inside it — a single icon (no tooltip configured) now centers in the pill instead of sitting off to one side
Rules tab: a collapsed rule no longer looks like a bordered title box nested inside a bigger card — the whole header now expands/collapses like a main-panel group header, with no separate arrow

Rules tab: replaced the Delete button with a ⋮ menu (Rename / Duplicate / Delete). Clicking a rule's row only expands or collapses it now; Rename is the only way to edit the title, and it opens the title as an editable field in place
Every Setup tab now has a small SVG icon next to its label
Right-click any Setup tab to choose how it displays: Icon, Word, or Icon + Word — saved per tab, applies immediately

Fixed a real bug: the Scan button's hotkey tooltip stayed blank on a fresh install (even though the default Ctrl+Shift+S shortcut was genuinely working) until you manually set a hotkey — a fallback logic mismatch between two places that compute the same thing

All tooltips now use the same sleek floating style as the group info icon, instead of the browser's plain default tooltip — applies to the Scan button, the hide-group icon, and a group's inline header message
Setup's scrollbar now matches the docked panel's (slim, dark, rounded)
Setup window is now resizable — drag the handle in the bottom-right corner

Setup's tabs now have a Chrome-style shape: rounded top corners, the active tab's background flows into the content area below it with a curved (not square) transition, and a light divider separates adjacent tabs except on either side of the active one

Fixed: a collapsed rule card in the Rules tab showed a noticeable empty gap around the header — the card's own padding wrapped the header even when the body below it was hidden. Padding now lives on the header and body separately.

Fixed: dragging the Setup window's title bar off-screen could make it unrecoverable, since the title bar is the only handle you can grab to drag it back. It's now fully contained top-to-bottom; up to 75% of its width can still go off the left or right edge.
Moved the status summary bar to sit between the scan log and the first group (was between the status line and the scan log)

Replaced the small per-rule dots in a group header with a single badge showing the group's worst rule status (error > fail > warn > pass, matching priority) — all four badges are the same fixed size regardless of label length
The badge shares the same spot as the tooltip/hide icons — hovering the header swaps the badge out for those icons rather than showing both at once. Groups with no rules attached show no badge.

Started the Setup window's visual overhaul (phase 2, after the docked panel) — same Signal look, scoped separately so it can't affect the panel. This round: the modal shell (title bar, tabs) and the Rules tab.

Setup's 11 buttons were one flat undifferentiated row with no indication of which tab was open. Regrouped into content tabs (Rules/Groups/Variables/Scan), management tabs (Profiles/Settings/Update), and lighter-weight utility actions (Guide/Export/Import) — and the active tab is now visibly highlighted.
Save & Apply moved into the title bar next to Close, instead of a green button lost among the other 10
Rules tab restyled to match: rule cards, the Pass/Fail/Warn message editor, formula boxes, and buttons all now use the same palette and controls as the docked panel

Groups, Variables, Scan, Profiles, Settings, Update, and Guide still use the old look for now — converting one tab at a time rather than one large unverifiable change, same approach as the panel redesign.
The panel can now be collapsed — a small handle on its left edge shrinks it to a thin strip and gives the screen back to Maximo, click again to expand. Remembered across reloads.

Changing the update Channel or Version in Setup > Settings no longer applies instantly (which could trigger an immediate install/reload) — it now stages the change like every other Setup tab and only takes effect when you click Save & Apply

Added a rule status summary bar under the status line — a quick pass/fail/warn/error count across every rule, since scrolling can hide most groups from view at once. On by default; a toggle in Setup > Settings > Display turns it off
The status line text is now a visible amber/yellow instead of muted gray

The hide-group icon and the tooltip icon are now hidden until you hover the group header, then appear in gray; hovering the icon itself now highlights white (previously hovering the hide icon turned it red)
"Will's WO" in the panel title is now bold white
Reduced the indent on rule messages in the expanded tile view — it was calibrated to align under a status dot that's no longer there
Styled the panel's own scrollbars (group list, scan log) to match the rest of the panel — slim, dark, rounded — instead of the browser's default scrollbar

Found the actual root cause of the scroll/squishing bug (comparing against the pre-redesign code that worked): the scrollable groups container had been changed to display:flex, which turns every group tile into a flex item — and flex items shrink to fit by default instead of the container scrolling past them. Reverted it to plain block layout (what the original working version used), so tiles now overflow and scroll correctly instead of getting squeezed.

Restructured the panel into three regions instead of one scrolling area: the title/status/scan-log always stay fixed at the top, the return message box and Return/Approve/Show-hidden buttons always stay fixed at the bottom, and only the group tiles in the middle scroll. The return message and action buttons are now always reachable without scrolling, no matter how many groups are expanded above them.

Fixed: the tooltip and hide icons in a group header sat at the left (next to the title) instead of the right whenever there was no header message showing — including always, pre-scan, since that's exactly when there's nothing to push them over. They now stay pinned right regardless.

Escalated the panel body's scroll behavior to !important — two prior attempts at plain (non-!important) inline styles still wasn't enough to make expanding multiple groups scroll instead of cutting content off, which points at something on the Maximo page forcing scroll/overflow behavior globally
Fixed a real regression from 0.20.0/0.20.1: the status line and scan log had no background at all, so the Maximo page showed through them ("transparent status section")

Fixed panel body scrolling — expanding multiple groups no longer squishes/cuts off content or hides the return-message box at the bottom. The scroll/background properties on the panel's core structure are now set directly rather than through the shared stylesheet, so they can't be affected by anything on the host page

Fixed a real layout bug: the panel body was missing a flex min-height, so once content overflowed it grew/squished instead of scrolling — expanding rows no longer clips the status area or loses the scrollbar
Group headers are now a consistent height whether collapsed or expanded, and content is vertically centered correctly in both states
Removed the separate collapse arrow button — the whole header is now the collapse control (still fully keyboard-accessible: Tab to it, Enter/Space to toggle)
The header's inline rule message now pins to the right and gets all the space left over after the title, instead of crowding the title on the left — the title itself is never truncated
Replaced the tooltip ⓘ glyph with a properly sized/centered icon

Removed the redundant colored dot and "Passed"/"Failed"/"Warning" label inside each rule row where a message is already shown below it — the colored left edge of the row is the status indicator; kept the label in the few cases where it's the only information (no configured message)

New visual style for the docked panel ("Signal"): higher-contrast dark theme, bigger hit targets, full-width status coloring instead of small dots alone — chosen to be easy to scan quickly, not just prettier
Group tiles now collapse/expand from a click anywhere on the header, not just the small arrow button (the arrow still works too)
The small "x" that hid a group tile is now a faint eye icon that brightens on hover — same action (hide this group), clearer intent, less visually noisy at rest
Removed decorative emoji from status messages and banners (pin/refresh/checkmark/folder icons) in favor of plain text — the meaningful ✓ / ✗ / ⚠ status symbols are unchanged

All docked-panel styling now comes from a single stylesheet scoped to the tool's own panel, instead of styles written inline on every element — makes the look consistent and easier to adjust going forward
This covers the docked panel only. Setup's tabs (Rules, Groups, Variables, Scan, Profiles, Settings, Update) keep their current look for now — same visual system is coming to those next.

Fixed: "Re-import & Switch" on a GitHub config profile (Setup > Profiles) had no confirmation and no way to recover — it silently overwrote your current config with the GitHub version, discarding any local edits. It now asks for confirmation and automatically saves whatever you had as a backup profile first, so nothing is lost even if you click through it

Patch-level updates (same X.Y line, e.g. 0.19.0 → 0.19.1) now auto-install silently by default — bug fixes reach everyone without needing a Setup visit. Minor/major updates (new features, behavior changes) still always prompt unless a separate, off-by-default setting is turned on. Both are toggleable in Setup > Settings > Updates

Added floating minor-version pinning: pin to "0.19.x (auto-patch)" to stay on a specific feature line forever while always taking its newest patch, instead of freezing at one exact build. Exact-version pinning (e.g. "0.19.0") still exists underneath as the rollback escape hatch if a specific patch turns out to be bad

Fixed a subtle case in the pin-reconciliation logic added in 0.16.0: installing a floating pin's own newest patch no longer silently converts it into an exact pin (which would have frozen it and stopped future patches from reaching it)

Fixed a real bug: scan-step conditions were evaluated against an empty cache before any tab was read, so any condition checking a Work Order field (e.g. Lot #, Work Type) always resolved false — Approvers and Downtime steps in the default config were never actually running

Scan conditions are now evaluated one step at a time, right before that step runs, so a later step's condition can see data captured by an earlier step in the same scan (not just what was on-screen when the scan started)
Added reorder controls (▲▼) to Setup > Scan — scan steps run in the order shown, so if tab C's condition depends on data from tab B, B now needs to come first, and you can now actually arrange that

Redesigned rule messages: Pass, Fail, and Warn are now each a Short (one line, for the group header) and Long (a list of one-or-more messages, each with its own optional condition) — all edited inline in the Rules tab

Fixed a real corruption bug: fail/warn message conditions used to be parsed from a single textarea by splitting on the first ' :: ', which collided with qualified field names like "Tab :: Field" (the exact string the field-insert dropdown produces) — condition and message are now separate input fields, so this can no longer happen
Pass messages now support the same multi-entry, conditional list as Fail/Warn (previously a single string)
Per-rule "include in return message" (None/Short/Long/Custom) moved from Settings > Quick Return Message into each rule's Fail/Warn section — Settings still holds the shared prefix/suffix/delimiter

Existing configs migrate automatically on load — old-shape rules (in a saved profile, a restored backup, or a stale pinned version's data) are normalized every time they're read, so this can never silently break by loading old data after updating
configs/default.json and configs/maintenance.json migrated to the new schema; one real corrupted rule (a fail-message condition truncated by the ' :: ' bug) was found and repaired in the process
Fixed: a linked PC backup file now saves ALL local config profiles, not just whichever one is currently active — switching profiles or restoring on a new machine brings everything back
Added "Start Blank Profile" in Setup > Profiles, to build a config from scratch as a new named profile instead of only ever cloning the current one
The Delete button in Setup > Profiles is now clearly greyed out (with a tooltip explaining why) instead of just being unresponsive when it's disabled

Fixed: pinning to a version while an update banner was showing could install it, then get silently reverted back to the old pin on the next check — any explicit install now reconciles the pin instead of fighting it
Fixed: re-pinning to a newer version after pinning down to an older one now works reliably (previously could appear permanently stuck)
The update banner no longer lingers after a pin/channel change — it's dismissed immediately, and never shown at all while a pin is active (just a status line, e.g. "Pinned to v0.15.0")
Fixed: the Setup window could survive a code hot-reload and go stale/disconnected, making the tool feel unresponsive after an update — it now always closes cleanly first
Added a first-run installer shown on brand-new installs — pick a starting config profile (and channel, if unlocked) instead of a silent default
Added config profiles: named, portable config subsets (rules/scan/fields/vars) that can be saved, switched, and imported from GitHub — Setup > Profiles
Config is now split into configs/default.json and configs/maintenance.json (was a single root config.json), each fetchable independently as a preset

Profile switching merges only config-level settings (message templates, rule return messages, auto-scan) — it never touches device-level settings like update channel, pinned version, auto-backup, or hotkey
Added window.__woShowInstaller()/__woReset() console helpers for exercising the first-run flow without manually wiping storage

Fixed: fresh installs on a new browser now seed from the GitHub-hosted config.json default instead of a bare hardcoded fallback — custom rules, groups, variables, scan steps, and rule messages now arrive out of the box
Added a built-in default scan hotkey (Ctrl+Shift+S) that applies whenever no config has set one, regardless of which config/profile is loaded
Fixed a landmine where seeding from config.json could have silently downgraded a fresh install back to an old cached code version — code is now never sourced from a config file
Consolidated the channel/version-pin controls into the existing Settings → Updates section instead of a separate box
Fixed: Update Channel is now visible to everyone — all users can pick Stable or pin to any released version
Only the Beta and Dev channel options (and beta/dev-tagged builds in the pin list) stay hidden until window.__woEnableBeta()/__woEnableDev() is run in the console
Added tag-pinned update channels (stable/beta/dev) so installs can be pinned or rolled back to any prior version
dev channel tracks the tip of main directly; stable/beta pull from immutable git tags
Failed or corrupted update downloads now always fall back to the currently running version instead of risking a broken install
Added post-scan actions — fill fields automatically after each tab is scanned
Added auto-backup to local file (Chrome/Edge)
Added startup config restore if localStorage is wiped
Added cross-browser config sync via linked backup file
Added Variables feature — define computed values using field/table data
Variables can be shown inline in group field rows
Added variable display in group header messages
Fixed sel.onchange missing bug in Setup > Groups field row editor
